report_name: Living Off the Land: Threat Actors Increasingly Abuse Legitimate Platforms Like Teams and GitHub

attack_patterns.name: Remote Access Software
attack_patterns.description: Attackers abuse legitimate remote access and synchronization software, such as Microsoft Teams and Windows Phone Link, to deliver malware and intercept data, blending their activities with normal user behavior.

attack_patterns.name: Web Service
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use legitimate web services like GitHub for hosting malware, C2 configurations, and as distribution points, leveraging the platform's trusted reputation to evade detection.

attack_patterns.name: Spearphishing Link
attack_patterns.description: Malicious links are delivered through Microsoft Teams chat, exploiting the implicit trust users have in the internal communication platform to bypass traditional email security controls.

attack_patterns.name: Multi-Factor Authentication Interception
attack_patterns.description: The Windows Phone Link application is exploited on a compromised PC to intercept SMS-based MFA one-time passcodes (OTPs) that are synced from the user's phone, allowing for account takeover.

attack_patterns.name: Application Layer Protocol
attack_patterns.description: Public blockchains are used for command-and-control (C2) by embedding commands or exfiltrated data into transaction metadata, creating a resilient and difficult-to-block C2 channel.

tools.name: Microsoft Teams
tools.description: Abused by threat actors for malware delivery through chat messages, bypassing traditional email security gateways by leveraging the platform's trusted status within organizations.

tools.name: GitHub
tools.description: Used by attackers to host malware, command-and-control configurations, and as a distribution point for malicious payloads, leveraging its reputation to evade detection.

tools.name: Windows Phone Link
tools.description: Exploited by financially motivated cybercrime groups to intercept SMS-based multi-factor authentication (MFA) one-time passcodes (OTPs) from a compromised Windows PC, enabling account takeover.

tools.name: Blockchain
tools.description: Used as a command-and-control (C2) channel by embedding commands or data into transaction metadata, creating a highly resilient and difficult-to-block communication method.

infrastructure.name: Microsoft Teams as Malware Delivery Vector
infrastructure.description: Microsoft Teams is used as a trusted channel to deliver malicious files and links directly to users, bypassing email gateways.

infrastructure.name: GitHub as Malware Distribution Point
infrastructure.description: GitHub repositories are used to host malware, C2 configurations, and compromised installers, leveraging the platform's trusted status.

infrastructure.name: Blockchain as C2 Channel
infrastructure.description: Public blockchains are used for C2 by embedding commands or data in transaction metadata, creating a resilient and difficult-to-block channel.

indicators.name: Windows Phone Link Execution
indicators.x_netsecio_sigma_rule:
title: Windows Phone Link Execution
id: f1a2b3c4-d5e6-4789-a0b1-c2d3e4f5a6b7
status: experimental
description: Detects the execution of the Windows Phone Link application (PhoneExperienceHost.exe). While legitimate, its presence can indicate an attack surface for SMS-based MFA interception if the host is compromised.
references:
  - https://www.resecurity.com/blog/threat-intelligence-may-11-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1219
level: low
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: '\PhoneExperienceHost.exe'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the execution of the Windows Phone Link application. While legitimate, its presence on a compromised host creates an attack surface for SMS MFA interception.

indicators.name: Suspicious File Transfer in Microsoft Teams
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious File Type Shared in Microsoft Teams
id: a1b2c3d4-e5f6-4789-a0b1-c2d3e4f5a6b7
status: experimental
description: Detects the sharing of executable files or archives in Microsoft Teams chats. This is a common TTP for malware delivery that bypasses email gateways.
references:
  - https://www.resecurity.com/blog/threat-intelligence-may-11-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1566.002
level: medium
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.teams
detection:
  selection:
    Operation: 'FileShared'
    FileName|endswith:
      - '.exe'
      - '.dll'
      - '.scr'
      - '.zip'
      - '.rar'
      - '.7z'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: Detects the sharing of executable files or archives in Microsoft Teams, a common TTP for malware delivery.

indicators.name: Suspicious File Download from GitHub
indicators.pattern:
title: Suspicious File Download from GitHub
id: b1c2d3e4-f5a6-4789-a0b1-c2d3e4f5a6b7
status: experimental
description: Detects the download of executable files or archives from GitHub. This can indicate malware distribution leveraging GitHub's trusted status.
references:
  - https://www.resecurity.com/blog/threat-intelligence-may-11-2026
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-12
tags:
  - attack.t1102
level: medium
logsource:
  category: proxy
detection:
  selection:
    cs-host|endswith: 'github.com'
    c-uri|contains: '/releases/download/'
    c-uri|endswith:
      - '.exe'
      - '.msi'
      - '.dll'
      - '.zip'
      - '.rar'
  filter_legit:
    c-useragent|contains:
      - 'winget'
      - 'chocolatey'
  condition: selection and not filter_legit
indicators.description: Detects downloads of executables or archives from GitHub release assets, which could indicate malware staging.

relationships.source_name: Suspicious File Transfer in Microsoft Teams
